name: Keanna Fogah
ai_summary: Keanna Fogah is a 30-year-old early childhood education certified caregiver from Jamaica with daycare and teaching experience. Based in College Park, GA with full-time availability, she resolves conflicts by intervening, separating children, and teaching them to use words to ask for toys.

transcripts.q3: Hi. My name is Kenneth hoga and I'm 30 years old, and I'm from Jamaica. And I have two years experience working with childcare. In my home country, I did a certificate program in early childhood learning and I also did my teaching practice and that elementary school that I dealt with kids from Age, 4 to 9 years old. And after my teaching practice, I got employment at a daycare where I dealt with mostly toddlers for a year after that, I migrated here to America, where I've been helping friends, Friends family coworkers at other job, you know, with their kids and stuff like that. And I believe that working with children is a rewarding career. It helps your helping to Foster growth and development and you experience a genuine sense of joy, you know working with kids you help to be a good role model to influence their decision from a young age and you also We'll get to create a lasting bond with them.
transcripts.q4: I'm currently located in College Park, Georgia and I am willing to go within this area and also surrounding cities within like 20 mile radius to work.
transcripts.q5: I am currently looking for a full-time Monday. Through Friday, from opening hours, 25 5:30 in the evenings.
transcripts.q6: Okay. So working within a day care, I've had a situation where one child hit another child for a toy. So what I did was to quickly intervene separated them so they wouldn't get in a fight and let them know that hitting each other is not acceptable and they are to use their words when trying to get something from someone else like to kindly ask if they could use the toy eyes. I sat both of them down after they have calm down and had them. Ask each other, take turns and ask each other if they could use the toy.
transcripts.q7: I would make a great kid teacher because this is a career that I enjoy. I have a genuine love for children. This is something I enjoy doing. I didn't choose this career, just because of money or just because it's another job. I chose this because this is something I genuinely love. I love having influence, good influence on children, you know, helping them to make good decisions at all. Early age, you know, to shake their mind from a very young age especially because you know of the world that we live in now. And I also like to be a good role model. I like to say good examples to children. Like I said, it's a, it's a really rewarding career and this is just something that I genuinely love.
